title: File Explorer Advanced
subtitle: organize, search, manage!
overview: This session helps students learn advanced features of File Explorer such as creating folders, managing files, searching files, renaming, copying, moving, deleting, and understanding storage organization in Windows.

activity.title: Folder Organization Activity
steps: Open File Explorer using Windows + E
Create a folder named 'My School Work'
Inside it create folders: Maths, Science, English
Copy files into correct subject folders
Delete one file and restore it from Recycle Bin
Search for a file using the search bar

quiz.q: What is the shortcut key to open File Explorer?
quiz.explanation: Windows + E opens File Explorer quickly.

quiz.q: What is used to organize files?
quiz.explanation: Folders help organize and store files properly.

quiz.q: Which option is used to duplicate a file?
quiz.explanation: Copy creates another version of the file.

quiz.q: Where do deleted files go first?
quiz.explanation: Deleted files are stored temporarily in the Recycle Bin.
Rename changes the name of a file or folder.

quiz.q: Which drive usually contains Windows system files?
quiz.explanation: C: Drive is commonly the main system drive.

quiz.q: Which feature helps find files quickly?
quiz.explanation: Search Bar helps users quickly locate files and folders.
Cut + Paste moves files from one place to another.

quiz.q: Which view shows file details like size and date?
quiz.explanation: Details view shows file information clearly.
Proper organization helps users manage and find files quickly.
